data.title: House Republicans introduce digital asset market clarity act
data.description:
House Republicans introduced the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act to create a clear regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies in the U. S.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/house-gop-unveils-digital-asset-clarity-act/

data.title: Coinbase to reopen office in San Francisco
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: San Francisco Mayor Daniel Lurie said on the X platform: "Coinbase is returning to San Francisco after leaving it four years ago and has opened a 150,000 square foot office in Mission Rock. San Francisco is an ideal place to start and grow a company. Welcome back." In response, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said: "We are very happy to reopen an office in San Francisco. Although there is still a lot of work to do to improve the city."

data.title: US SEC clarifies that some PoS staking activities do not constitute securities transactions
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Eleanor Terrett, a crypto journalist, the SEC’s Division of Corporate Finance clarified that staking activities on some Proof-of-Stake blockchains do not constitute securities transactions.
This new guidance follows the guidance on some Proof-of-Work mining activities in March this year, covering self-staking, staking-as-a-service, and additional services such as slashing protection and custom rewards.
“Providing security itself is not a security,” said Hester Peirce, Commissioner of the SEC.

data.title: Tether CEO: The company holds more than 100,000 bitcoins and 50 tons of gold
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Paolo Ardoino, CEO of stablecoin issuer Tether, disclosed at the Bitcoin 2025 Conference that the company currently holds more than 100,000 bitcoins (worth more than $10 billion) and more than 50 tons of gold. According to the latest financial report, as of the end of the first quarter of 2025, Tether held bitcoins worth more than $7 billion (based on the then $83,000 per coin), and physical gold bars worth more than $6 billion.
Ardoino said that Tether achieved a profit of about $13 billion in 2024, making it the most profitable company in the crypto industry. Regarding the decision to hold gold, he explained that "gold is not competing with Bitcoin, but with fiat currency." Currently, USDT is the world's largest dollar stablecoin. (The Block)

data.title: Micro-Brazil International plans to raise up to $300 million to build XRP reserves to support its global payment solutions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Mobile travel service provider Webus International Limited is exploring a financing strategy of up to US$300 million to establish an XRP reserve to support its global payment solutions through diversified non-equity financing tools. The strategy may include the use of available cash reserves, bank loans, shareholder guarantees, and third-party credit support from traditional banks. By adopting these non-dilutive financing methods, the company aims to maintain capital flexibility, support long-term growth, and protect shareholder value while advancing its digital asset and cross-border payment strategic goals.

data.title: Ethereum Games 'Realms of Alurya' and 'Wonder Wars' Latest to Go Offline
data.description:
Realms of Alurya and Wonder Wars—both building on Ethereum network Ronin—are the latest crypto games to go offline in a grim, growing trend.
source: https://decrypt.co/322844/ethereum-games-realms-alurya-wonder-wars-offline

data.title: US SEC applies to the court to withdraw the lawsuit against Binance
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. SEC has submitted documents to the court requesting the withdrawal of the lawsuit against the cryptocurrency trading platform Binance and its former CEO Zhao Changpeng. The U.S. SEC, Binance, and lawyers for Binance founder CZ signed a joint withdrawal agreement and submitted it to the federal court in Washington, DC. And said that based on policy considerations, it is appropriate to withdraw the case.
The lawsuit began in 2023, and the SEC accused Binance of unregistered operations and misrepresentation of trading control. In 2023, Binance pleaded guilty to violating the Bank Secrecy Act and paid a fine of $4.3 billion. The documents show that the SEC's newly established crypto working group has recently held extensive consultations with the industry. The SEC has recently withdrawn lawsuits against several crypto companies such as Coinbase and Uniswap. SEC Commissioner Peirce said that law enforcement should target clear violations, but will still crack down on violations in the crypto field. (The Block)

data.title: Paris Saint-Germain Football Club Announces It Has Included Bitcoin in Its Financial Reserves
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Paris Saint-Germain Football Club (PSG) of France announced that it has included Bitcoin in its financial reserves. Pär Helgosson, head of PSG Labs, revealed at the Bitcoin 2025 Conference in Las Vegas that the club began to buy and hold Bitcoin last year, becoming the first top football club to publicly hold Bitcoin. Helgosson said that PSG converted part of its fiat currency reserves into Bitcoin and still holds these assets. As one of the world's largest football clubs, PSG became the first institution in the sports world to publicly hold Bitcoin. The club also plans to invest in "Bitcoin entrepreneurs" and "Bitcoin projects" and use its 550 million global fan base to help these projects expand their markets. (The Block)

data.title: Fed's Daly: If labor market remains strong and inflation falls, two rate cuts this year are reasonable
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Daly said that if the labor market remains strong and inflation falls, it is reasonable to cut interest rates twice this year, but the scope of potential risks is still large. (Jinshi)

data.title: Arthur Hayes: ETH may rise to $5,000 by the end of the year, setting a new record high
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes believes that Ethereum may rise to $5,000 by the end of the year, almost doubling its price and setting a new record high, although the asset has recently lagged behind other competing projects such as Bitcoin and Solana.
Arthur Hayes told Decrypt at the Bitcoin 2025 conference in Las Vegas, "I think ETH may rise to $4,000 to $5,000 this year. I think it is the most hated L1 public chain at present, and usually when the market cycle reverses, you should hold the most hated assets, so I think ETH may perform better than others."
Arthur Hayes' latest forecast also continues his bullish stance on Bitcoin. He said earlier this month that the price of Bitcoin may rise to $1 million by 2028.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Comments on Trade Partner Cooperation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant stated that trade partners are cooperating in good faith, with no change in their attitude over the past 48 hours. Several significant agreements are expected to be finalized soon.

data.title: SEC Official Emphasizes Investor Responsibility in Cryptocurrency Decisions
data.description:
According to PANews, Hester Peirce, head of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) crypto working group, stated at the Bitcoin 2025 conference that cryptocurrency investors should be accountable for their investment decisions rather than seeking government assistance in the event of losses. Peirce emphasized that those advocating for free choice should learn from investment failures instead of demanding bailouts.

Peirce noted that most crypto tokens are not securities, and related trading platforms do not need to register with the SEC unless they engage in securities-related activities. Regarding the recent popularity of meme coins, she clearly stated that this is a speculative area where investors must assume their own risks. On the issue of publicly traded companies holding crypto assets, Peirce maintained a neutral stance, stressing the importance of proper information disclosure.

Recently, the SEC has issued a policy statement excluding meme coins, certain mining activities, and stablecoins from its regulatory scope. Peirce mentioned that the SEC is working to clarify the regulatory boundaries for digital asset securities, but a specific regulatory framework for retail crypto trading still requires congressional legislation. When asked whether federal crypto regulation is necessary, the audience responded with a clear opposition.

data.title: SEC Dismisses Binance Lawsuit in Policy Shift
data.description:
SEC ends Binance lawsuit, citing policy considerations; impacts market and regulatory strategies.
source: https://coincu.com/340582-sec-dismisses-binance-lawsuit-policy-shift

data.title: Panama City Explores Bitcoin Payment Initiatives Inspired by El Salvador
data.description:
According to PANews, Panama City's Mayor Mayer Mizrachi announced at the Bitcoin 2025 conference that the city is considering adopting a Bitcoin payment system similar to El Salvador's model. The city has already passed legislation allowing residents to pay municipal fees, such as taxes and parking, using Bitcoin, Ethereum, and certain stablecoins. Mizrachi highlighted plans to offer priority passage through the Panama Canal for ships paying tolls with Bitcoin and is contemplating establishing a municipal Bitcoin reserve. This initiative is directly inspired by El Salvador's President Bukele, whose country became the first to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender four years ago.

The Panama Canal Authority has yet to formally respond to the cryptocurrency payment proposal. The canal generated $4.9 billion in toll revenue in the 2024 fiscal year, handling an average of 30-40 ships daily.

data.title: Commissioner Peirce warns crypto investors not to expect bailouts from SEC, crypto mom
data.description:
SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce, fondly called crypto mom, stated that the SEC withdrawing from over-enforcement does not mean it will not move in cases of clear violations in the crypto space. Under new leadership, the U. S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has decided to drop several crypto-related lawsuits and probes. However, that doesn’t mean the […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/peirce-crypto-warns-investors-no-bailouts/
